description: Curated nutrigenomics SNP panel for GenomeInsight (P3-08). Covers 6 nutrient pathways with evidence-gated categorical scoring.

pathways.name: Folate Metabolism
pathways.description: Variants affecting folate/homocysteine metabolism via the one-carbon pathway.

GG.effect_summary: Normal MTHFR enzyme activity. Standard folate metabolism.

GA.effect_summary: Reduced MTHFR enzyme activity (~65% of normal). Mildly elevated homocysteine possible.

AG.effect_summary: Reduced MTHFR enzyme activity (~65% of normal). Mildly elevated homocysteine possible.

AA.effect_summary: Significantly reduced MTHFR enzyme activity (~30% of normal). Consider dietary folate sources and discuss methylfolate supplementation with a clinician.

snps.recommendation_text: Consider dietary folate sources (leafy greens, legumes) and discuss methylfolate supplementation with a clinician.

AA.effect_summary: Normal MTHFR enzyme activity at A1298C locus.

AC.effect_summary: Mildly reduced MTHFR enzyme activity at A1298C locus.

CA.effect_summary: Mildly reduced MTHFR enzyme activity at A1298C locus.

CC.effect_summary: Moderately reduced MTHFR enzyme activity at A1298C locus. Effect is less pronounced than C677T.

snps.recommendation_text: Effect is milder than C677T. Compound heterozygosity (C677T + A1298C) may have additive effects on homocysteine levels.

AG.effect_summary: Mildly reduced methionine synthase reductase activity. May contribute to elevated homocysteine in combination with MTHFR variants.

GA.effect_summary: Mildly reduced methionine synthase reductase activity. May contribute to elevated homocysteine in combination with MTHFR variants.

snps.recommendation_text: Supportive role in folate metabolism. Effect is primarily relevant in combination with MTHFR variants.

pathways.name: Vitamin D
pathways.description: Variants affecting vitamin D synthesis, transport, and metabolism.

TT.effect_summary: Normal vitamin D binding protein levels.

TG.effect_summary: Modestly lower vitamin D binding protein levels. May have mildly reduced circulating 25(OH)D.

GT.effect_summary: Modestly lower vitamin D binding protein levels. May have mildly reduced circulating 25(OH)D.

GG.effect_summary: Significantly lower vitamin D binding protein levels. Associated with reduced circulating 25(OH)D.

snps.recommendation_text: Consider vitamin D status monitoring and adequate sun exposure or supplementation as directed by a clinician.

AA.effect_summary: Normal vitamin D binding protein variant.

AC.effect_summary: Modestly reduced vitamin D binding affinity.

CA.effect_summary: Modestly reduced vitamin D binding affinity.

CC.effect_summary: Reduced vitamin D binding affinity. Associated with lower circulating 25(OH)D.

snps.recommendation_text: May contribute to lower vitamin D levels. Discuss with a clinician if deficiency is suspected.

AA.effect_summary: Reduced CYP2R1 expression. May contribute to lower vitamin D levels.

snps.recommendation_text: Supportive variant for vitamin D pathway assessment.

pathways.name: Vitamin B12
pathways.description: Variants affecting vitamin B12 absorption and transport.

GG.effect_summary: Non-secretor genotype. Associated with reduced vitamin B12 absorption from food sources.

GA.effect_summary: Secretor genotype. Normal vitamin B12 absorption.

AG.effect_summary: Secretor genotype. Normal vitamin B12 absorption.

AA.effect_summary: Secretor genotype. Normal vitamin B12 absorption.

snps.recommendation_text: Non-secretors may benefit from monitoring B12 levels, particularly on plant-based diets.

CT.effect_summary: Modestly lower circulating vitamin B6 levels.

TC.effect_summary: Modestly lower circulating vitamin B6 levels.

snps.recommendation_text: Consider dietary sources of vitamin B6 (poultry, fish, potatoes, chickpeas).

pathways.name: Omega-3 Fatty Acids
pathways.description: Variants affecting omega-3 and omega-6 fatty acid desaturation and metabolism.

TT.effect_summary: Reduced FADS1 desaturase activity. Lower conversion of ALA to EPA/DHA. Dietary omega-3 intake or supplementation may be more important.

TC.effect_summary: Mildly reduced FADS1 desaturase activity. Intermediate conversion of ALA to EPA/DHA.

CT.effect_summary: Mildly reduced FADS1 desaturase activity. Intermediate conversion of ALA to EPA/DHA.

CC.effect_summary: Normal FADS1 desaturase activity. Efficient conversion of ALA to EPA/DHA.

snps.recommendation_text: Consider direct dietary sources of EPA/DHA (fatty fish, algae-based supplements) if desaturase activity is reduced.

AG.effect_summary: Modestly altered omega-6 fatty acid levels.

GA.effect_summary: Modestly altered omega-6 fatty acid levels.

GG.effect_summary: Altered omega-6 fatty acid levels. Associated with higher inflammatory omega-6 to omega-3 ratio.

snps.recommendation_text: Balance omega-6 and omega-3 intake. Consider increasing omega-3-rich foods.

pathways.name: Iron Metabolism
pathways.description: Variants affecting iron absorption and storage, particularly hereditary hemochromatosis risk.

GG.effect_summary: No HFE C282Y mutation. Normal iron regulation at this locus.

GA.effect_summary: Carrier of HFE C282Y. Mildly increased iron absorption. Hemochromatosis risk depends on second HFE allele.

AG.effect_summary: Carrier of HFE C282Y. Mildly increased iron absorption. Hemochromatosis risk depends on second HFE allele.

AA.effect_summary: Homozygous HFE C282Y. Significantly increased iron absorption. Discuss iron status monitoring with a clinician.

snps.recommendation_text: Homozygous individuals should discuss iron level monitoring (serum ferritin, transferrin saturation) with a clinician.

CC.effect_summary: No HFE H63D mutation. Normal iron regulation at this locus.

CG.effect_summary: Carrier of HFE H63D. Minimal clinical significance alone. May contribute to iron overload risk with compound C282Y/H63D genotype.

GC.effect_summary: Carrier of HFE H63D. Minimal clinical significance alone.

GG.effect_summary: Homozygous HFE H63D. Mildly increased iron absorption. Lower penetrance than C282Y.

snps.recommendation_text: H63D alone has low clinical penetrance. Most relevant in compound heterozygosity with C282Y.

pathways.name: Lactose Tolerance
pathways.description: Variants affecting lactase persistence and lactose digestion in adulthood.

AA.effect_summary: Lactase persistent genotype. Likely able to digest lactose in adulthood.

AG.effect_summary: Lactase persistent genotype (one functional allele). Likely able to digest lactose in adulthood.

GA.effect_summary: Lactase persistent genotype (one functional allele). Likely able to digest lactose in adulthood.

GG.effect_summary: Lactase non-persistent genotype. Likely reduced ability to digest lactose in adulthood.

snps.recommendation_text: Lactase non-persistent individuals may benefit from lactose-free dairy products or lactase enzyme supplements.

TT.effect_summary: Lactase persistent genotype at secondary regulatory locus.

TC.effect_summary: Lactase persistent genotype at secondary regulatory locus.

CT.effect_summary: Lactase persistent genotype at secondary regulatory locus.

CC.effect_summary: Supports lactase non-persistence. Confirms finding from rs4988235 if both present.

snps.recommendation_text: Supportive variant for lactose tolerance assessment. Most informative when combined with rs4988235.

TCF7L2.note: TCF7L2 rs7903146 is included in GWAS Catalog data for Type 2 diabetes risk factor. Cross-referenced from metabolic GWAS findings but not a primary nutrigenomics pathway variant.

PPARG.note: PPARG Pro12Ala (rs1801282) relates to insulin sensitivity and fat metabolism. Referenced via GWAS associations.

MC4R.note: MC4R variants affect appetite regulation and BMI. Referenced via GWAS associations for obesity-related traits.

FTO.note: FTO rs9939609 is a well-replicated obesity/BMI variant. Referenced via GWAS associations.

APOE.note: APOE lipid/dietary findings are generated by the dedicated APOE module (P3-22). Not scored in the nutrigenomics pathway cards to avoid duplication.
